threat_model: Invariant fuzz evidence can accidentally become live runtime fuzzing if deterministic case-bank evidence, staging or production target refs, canonical state mutation authority, runtime lawbook migration, rollback execution, and terminal closure are conflated. The main risks are touching a live runtime target, mutating canonical state, leaking a secret probe through public projection, retaining raw case payloads or secrets, calling a connector during validation, bypassing UAO, and claiming runtime-hardening closure without rollback or incident handoff evidence. The mitigation is a dry-run-only schema and validator that reject live target drift, canonical mutation authority, projection leak drift, raw retention, missing oracle refs, and count drift.

required_checks.check: live runtime execution, staging or production targeting, canonical mutation, event-chain mutation, runtime lawbook migration, rollback execution, terminal closure, and success authority remain false
live runtime target refs remain denied by none:// dry-run target scope
projection and redaction checks confirm raw case payloads and raw secret material remain denied
policy bypass check confirms Foundation Mode, dry-run-only mode, strict baseline, and projection redaction remain enforced
receipt integrity check confirms UAO, LifeMeaningJudgment, EffectAssurance, SimulationReceipt, WorkerFailureReceipt, and SDLC recovery refs are retained
audit visibility check confirms case-bank, oracle, result, and summary counts cannot drift
audit visibility check confirms the proof coverage matrix includes the InvariantFuzzExecutionReport surface

findings.mitigation: Foundation Mode report blocks live runtime execution, staging or production targeting, canonical state mutation, event-chain mutation, runtime lawbook migration, connector calls, writes, rollback execution, terminal closure, and success claims.

Foundation Mode report requires projection leak checking, public projection checked status, zero projection leaks, case-bank digest refs, result-bank digest refs, and raw case payload and raw secret retention denial.

mitigations: keep InvariantFuzzExecutionReport validation non-live and deterministic
require deterministic seed refs, mutation-class refs, oracle refs, expected accept counts, expected reject counts, and projection probe counts
require case-bank and result-bank digest refs instead of raw case payloads
deny staging and production targets in Foundation Mode
deny canonical mutation, event-chain mutation, runtime lawbook migration, connector calls, writes, rollback execution, terminal closure, and success claims
